name: Chiba Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the deep seas surrounding the Boso Peninsula
purity and the prefecture's bright future
fertility, ripening grain, and the rising sun

description: A deep blue field charged at the centre with a white emblem (outlined in yellow) stylising the hiragana ち and ば of the prefecture's name.
history: Chiba's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and adopted on 18 October 1909, making it among the oldest prefectural symbols in Japan. The flag, with the white symbol on a blue field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since 1963.

features.symbolism: deep blue, representing the seas and skies of the Boso Peninsula

features.role: stylised hiragana ち and ば
features.symbolism: a stylised combination of the hiragana ち (chi) and ば (ba), forming the prefecture's name (ちば, Chiba). The form is sometimes also read as a leaf, evoking the kanji 葉 ('leaf') in 千葉 ('thousand leaves').
